question: Which of the following statements about a VPRN service label is TRUE?
options: It is pushed onto customer packets by the CE.
It is used to identify which VPRN a packet belongs to.
It contains the customer's IP address.
It is exchanged for a transport label during transport.
correct_answers: It is used to identify which VPRN a packet belongs to.

question: Which of the following statements about route distinguishers is FALSE?
options: A route distinguisher is an identifier used by the VPN-IPv4 family to ensure that IP prefixes are globally unique.
A route distinguisher is appended to an IPv4 prefix to form a 12-byte VPN-IPv4 prefix.
PE routers append route distinguishers to IPv4 prefixes received from local CEs over VPRN interfaces.
A VPRN DATA packet carries a route distinguisher in its VPN IPv4 destination address.
correct_answers: A route distinguisher is appended to an IPv4 prefix to form a 12-byte VPN-IPv4 prefix.

question: Which statement is FALSE regarding the traffic sent between two PE routers of a VPRN that uses RSVP-TE?
options: The traffic has an MPLS transport label.
The traffic has an MPLS service label.
The traffic has the Layer 2 header sent by the CE router.
The traffic has the IP header sent by the CE router.
None of the above statements is false.
correct_answers: None of the above statements is false.

question: Which of the following statements regarding an IES spoke termination to an epipe service is FALSE?
options: Targeted LDP is used to signal service labels for the IES spoke termination.
A SAP must be configured in the IES interface.
The service ID of the IES and epipe service can be different.
The VC ID between the spoke-SDP in the IES interface and the epipe must match.
correct_answers: The service ID of the IES and epipe service can be different.

question: Consider a VPRN configured on two Nokia 7750 SR PE routers, R1 and R2. R1 uses BGP for PE-CE routing; R2 uses static routes. Both sites share all routing information. Which statement is FALSE?
options: The BGP session between R1 and its CE router must be enabled for the IPv4 address family.
An export policy must be configured in the VPRN on R1 to advertise routes to its directly connected CE router.
An export policy is not required on R2 to advertise routes to the directly connected CE router.
An export policy must be configured to advertise VPN-IPv4 routes between the PE routers.
correct_answers: An export policy must be configured to advertise VPN-IPv4 routes between the PE routers.

question: Which of the following best describes the purpose of the route target?
options: The route target is added to the IPv4 or IPv6 prefix to create a unique VPN-IPv4 or VPN-IPv6 prefix.
The route target is used by the CE router to identify routes to be taken from its route table and advertised to the PE router.
The route target is used by the PE router to identify routes to be taken from MP-BGP and installed in the VRF.
The route target is used by the PE router to identify routes to be taken from the VRF and advertised to the CE router.
correct_answers: The route target is used by the PE router to identify routes to be taken from MP-BGP and installed in the VRF.

question: The 'show router bgp routes vpn-ipv4' command on router R1 shows that some MP-BGP routes have been received from R2, but they do not appear in the VRF as expected. What is the most likely reason?
options: The MP-BGP peering session between R1 and R2 is not operational.
The route distinguisher configured for the VPRN on R2 does not match the value configured on R1.
The transport tunnel between R1 and R2 is not operational.
The route distinguisher has not been configured in the VPRN on R2.
correct_answers: The route distinguisher has not been configured in the VPRN on R2.
